original: Herostratus sets fire to the Temple of Artemis in Ephesus, one of the Seven Wonders of the World
St Pontianus begins his reign as Catholic Pope
An earthquake on Crete followed by a tsunami around the Eastern Mediterranean allegedly destroys Alexandria
Pope Martin I is chosen as the successor of Theodore I
Holy Roman Emperor Louis III captured, blinded, and forced to relinquish his royal Italian and imperial crowns
Emperor Otto II gives earl Leopold I, East Bavaria
Pope Paul III begins inquisition against Protestants (Sactum Officium)
The first landing of French troops onto the coast of the Isle of Wight during the French invasion
Battle at Jemmingen: Alva's troops beat Dutch rebellion
Flemish city of Mechelen surrenders to Duke of Parma
First engagement between the English fleet and the Spanish Armada off the Eddystone Rocks
Spanish explorer Álvaro de Mendaña is the first European to discover the Marquesas Island in Eastern Polynesia
Manchu Prince Grogon issues edict that all Han Chinese men shave front of their head and wear hair in queues in the Manchu style
Treaty of Passarowitz is signed between the Ottoman Empire, Austria and the Republic of Venice
States of Holland put death penalty on "sodomy"
Pieter Steyn becomes pension advisor of Holland
Pope Clement XIV issues papal brief Dominus ac Redemptor, supressing the Jesuit order (lifted 1814)
Russo-Turkish War, 1768-74: Russia and the Ottoman Empire sign Treaty of Kuchuk-Kainarji ending the war
Java princess Dipo Negoro/Mangkubumi declare war on all non-islamics
Belgium gains independence from Netherlands, Leopold I made king
First Canadian public railway opens between La Prairie and Saint-Jean-sur-Richelieu, Quebec
Mormons found 1st English settlement in California (San Joaquin Valley)
First Battle of Bull Run [Battle of First Manassas], the first major battle of the US Civil War, is fought near Manassas, Virginia, and ends in a Confederate victory
Cholera epidemic kills hundreds in London
City Gardens on Folsom in San Francisco opens
US army breaks railroad strike
Compressed air accident kills 20 workers on Hudson River tunnel, NY
First Test cricket match is played at Lord's
National Federation of Colored Women established in Washington, D.C.
Tate Gallery opens in England
After 13 years, the 4,607-mile Trans-Siberian Railway is completed
Camille Jenatzy sets a world auto speed record at 65.79 mph (105.88 km/h)
The Egyptian government announces a new constitutional system and electoral law
Russian Revolution: Socialist Alexander Kerensky becomes Russian Prime Minister
U-156 shells Nauset Beach, in Orleans, Massachusetts
Dirigible crashes through bank skylight killing 13 in Chicago, Illinois
Irish Nationalist and Loyalists engage in street fighting over the issue of Irish independence from Britain, though Loyalist are reinforced by 1500 British Auxiliaries and 5800 British troops
MLB Cleveland Indians (9) and New York Yankees (7) combine to hit a record 16 doubles in 17-8 home team win at Dunn Field, Cleveland, Ohio
To prove his contention that air power is superior to sea power, US Colonel William Mitchell demonstrates how bombs from planes can sink a captured German battleship
Phillies score 12 in 6th and beat Cubs 17-4
110°F (43°C) at Millsboro, Delaware (state record)
US Veterans Administration forms
Reno racetrack becomes the first in the US to use daily double wagering
Haifa Harbor in Palestine opens
113°F (45°C), near Gallipolis, Ohio (state record)
Soviet Union annexes Estonia, Latvia, Lithuania
VARA-management accepts Rost of Tonningens demands
200 Jewish Torahs are burned in Ukraine
8 die as coal waste heap slides in river valley near Oakwood, Virginia
General Koiso becomes Premier of Japan
US forces land on Guam during its recapture from the Japanese (Operation Forager)
Detroit Tigers and Philadelphia A's play 24 inning 1-1 tie
Jesus T Pinerol becomes 1st native born Puerto Rican governor
Indonesia begins 1st political election
WSPD TV channel 13 in Toledo, OH (NBC) begins broadcasting
US Senate ratifies North Atlantic Treaty by a vote of 82-13 (NATO)
7.8 earthquake shakes Kern County California, 14 killed
Premier Ghavam es-Sultaneh of Persia, resigns

Geneva Accords for Indochina are signed, dividing French colonial territories into the countries of the Democratic Republic of Vietnam (North Vietnam), the State of Vietnam (South Vietnam), Cambodia, and Laos
USS Seawolf launches as the first submarine powered by a liquid metal-cooled nuclear reactor
Cincinnati Reds pitcher Brooks Lawrence loses after 13 straight wins
US performs atmospheric nuclear Test at Enewetak Atoll
1st nuclear powered merchant ship, NS Savannah, named, Camden NJ
Red Sox are last team to use a black player (Pumpsie Green)
Country of Katanga forms in Africa
NASA launches Mercury 4 (Liberty Bell) with Gus Grissom
160 civil right activists jailed after demonstration in Albany, Georgia
Battles on Chinese & Indies boundary
Arnold Long takes 11 catches in the match for Surrey v Sussex
Last Dutch whaling ship Willem Barents Sea sold to Japan
Race riots in Singapore between Chinese and Malay groups, 23 killed, 454 injured
Pakistan, Iran and Turkey sign Regional Co-Operation pact
Gemini X returns to Earth
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
Russia's Luna 15 impacts moon after 52 lunar orbits
Aswan High Dam opens in Egypt, enabling human control of the flooding of the Nile
Clay Kirby has a no-hitter going for 8 inn, but is lifted for a pinch hitter, Reliever Jack Baldschun gives up 3 hits & Padres lose, 3-0
Libya orders confiscation of all Jewish property
USSR performs nuclear test at the Semipalatinsk Test Site in northeast Kazakhstan
US performs nuclear test at Nevada Test Site
10.83 inches (27.5 cm) of rainfall at Fort Ripley, Minnesota (state record, until 2007)

Bloody Friday: within the space of 75 minutes, the Provisional Irish Republican Army explode twenty-two bombs in Belfast; six civilians, two British Army soldiers and one UDA volunteer were killed, 130 injured
In New York 57 murders occur in 24 hours
Two passenger trains collide head-on killing 76 (Seville, Spain)
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll in the Pacific
USSR launches Mars 4 for fly-by (2600 km) of red planet
Christopher Ewart-Biggs, British Ambassador to the Republic of Ireland, and his secretary Judith Cook are assassinated by a bomb planted by the Provisional IRA in his car in Dublin
First outbreak of Legionnaire's Disease kills 29 in Philadelphia
Libyan-Egyptian border fights
Sanjiva Reddy is elected sixth President of India unopposed
Sri Lankan Prime MinisterSirimavo Bandaranaikeloses general election
Bolivian military coup under General Juan Pereda, President Hugo Banzer flees
US Postal Service and unions agree on a contract averting mail strike
World's strongest dog, 80-kg St. Bernard, pulls a 2,909-kg load 27 meters
National Women's Hall of Fame, honoring American women is dedicated in Seneca Falls, NY
Jean-Claude Droyer climbs Eiffel Tower in 2 hrs 18 mins
Australia set 130 to win, all out 111 at Headingley Willis 8-43
France performs nuclear test at Mururoa Atoll
Polish government ends 19 months of martial law
US announces Lebanon freed American hostage David Dodge
World's lowest-ever natural temperature is recorded: -89.2°C (-128.6°F; 184.0 K) at the Soviet Vostok Station in Antarctica, surpassing record at same location set in 1960
Marita Koch of East Germany sets world women's mark for 200m, 21.71s
USSR performs underground nuclear Test
Amina Fakir (Detroit), 23, crowned 18th Miss Black America
Pleasure Island plans are unveiled as part of the Walt Disney World Village, now Disney Springs, in Florida
ESA's Ariane-3 launches 2 communications satellites (1 Indian)
Eastern Airlines submits a reorganization plan to creditors
Goodwill Games II opens in Seattle, Washington
Sharmell Sullivan (Gary Indiana), 20, crowned 23rd Miss Black America
KC Royals set club record of 22 singles in 15 innings
"The Daily Show," a late-night talk and satirical news program hosted by Craig Kilborn, premieres on Comedy Central in the US
NY Yank Mike Whiton held in Milwaukee on charges of sexual assault
The fully restored USS Constitution (aka "Old Ironsides") celebrates her 200th birthday, setting sail for the first time in 116 years
Telecom giant WorldCom files for Chapter 11 bankruptcy, the then largest such filing in United States history
The United Kingdom government publishes Delivering Security in a Changing World, a paper detailing wide-ranging reform of the country's armed forces
Four terrorist bombers target London's public transportation system, exactly two weeks after the July 7 bombings. All four bombs fail to detonate leading to the capture of all the bombers.
NASA's Space Shuttle program ends with the landing of Space Shuttle Atlantis on mission STS-135
12 people are killed in a clash between two Muslim families in Lanao del Sur, Philippines
15 Egyptian Army soldiers are killed after their bus crashes into a truck on the Mediterranean Coast Highway
After 3 weeks, the Israeli Defense Force (IDF) has struck about 2,800 targets in Gaza, while Gaza has fired 1,497 rockets at Israel
On This Day changes its domain name and brand from HistoryOrb.com to OnThisDay.com
White House Press Secretary Sean Spicer resigns after opposing appointment of Anthony Scaramucci
India scraps tax on sanitary products after campaign by activists
Governor of Puerto Rico Ricardo Rosselló says he won't seek re-election after widespread protests over misogynistic and homophobic comments in his leaked online chats
European Union agrees huge €750 billion ($859 billion) post-COVID-19 stimulus bill
Russian interference in British politics is 'the new normal' according to report by British Intelligence and Security Committee
Life expectancy in the US falls by 1.5 years (3 years for Hispanic Americans. 2.9 for Black Americans) in largest decline since WWII
World's fastest train debuts, a Chinese state-owned maglev bullet train with speeds of 600 kilometers per hour (373 miles per hour) in Qingdao, China
UK records temperatures over 40°C (104°F) for the first time (record 40.3°C at Coningsby) amid an extreme heatwave across much of Europe
2,000-year-old stone slab used to grind spices unearthed in Óc Eo, South Vietnam, is the earliest evidence of curry in Southeast Asia and still smells of nutmeg
Bangladesh's supreme court scraps government job quotas for families of its war of independence, that had led to widespread violent protests, killing at least 100 people
Hottest day on Earth: average global temperature of 17.09°C (62.76°F) surpasses July 6, 2023, record according to EU's Copernicus Climate Change Service; record is broken the next day
28 countries, including the UK, issue a joint statement calling for an immediate end to the Israeli war on Gaza, stating that the suffering of civilians has "reached new depths"
Diocletianappoints Maximian as Caesar and co-ruler

Battle of Shrewsbury: Army led by the LancastrianKing of England, Henry IVdefeats a rebel army led by Henry "Harry Hotspur" Percy of Northumberland thus ending the Percy challenge to the throne. Also the first battle English archers fought each other on English soil.
John Dudley, Lord President of the Council underEdward VI, captured in Cambridge
John Locke's Constitution of English colony Carolina is approved
Napoleon Bonapartewins Battle of Pyramids in Egypt (Battle of Embabeh) against Mamluk rulers, wiping out most of the Egyptian army
Central Park in New York createdwhen New York State Legislature puts aside more than 750 acres of land on Manhattan Island
In Springfield's market square in Missouri,Wild Bill Hickokshoots and kills Davis Tutt in what is regarded as the first true western showdown
Jesse Jamesand James Younger gang's 1st train robbery at Adair, Iowa
PopeLeo XIIIencyclical to Greek-Melkite rite
Woodrow Wilsonsends the third Lusitania note, warning Germany that future infringement of American rights will be deemed 'deliberately unfriendly'
Anthony Fokkerestablishes his new aircraft company, the Dutch Aircraft Factory in Amsterdam
Irish premier Eamon de Valera re-elected president of the Irish Dáil at the head of a Fianna Fáil government.
Paul Hindemithand Leonide Massine's ballet Nobilissima Visione premieres in London
Himmlerorders building of Majdanek concentration camp just outside the Polish city of Lublin
Musical film "Stormy Weather," directed by Andrew L. Stone and starringBill Robinson, Lena Horne, andFats Waller(singing "Ain't Misbehavin'"), premieres in the US
British premierWinston Churchillflies to France, meets Montgomery
Field MarshalGünther von KlugewarnsHitlerof impending collapse of front in Normandy
Dalai Lamareturns to Tibet
"The Quiet Man" film directed byJohn Fordand starringJohn WayneandMaureen O'Harais released in the UK
1st black to win a major US tennis tournament (Althea Gibson)
Francis Chichesterarrives in New York aboard Gypsy Moth II, setting a record of 40 days for a solo Atlantic crossing
Sirimavo Bandaranaikebecomes the world's first female elected head of government, as Prime Minister of Ceylon (now Sri Lanka)
American mobsterSam Giancanareturns to the United States after spending seven years of exile in Mexico
MLB Los Angeles Dodgers release knuckleball pitcherHoyt Wilhelm, ending his career
BravesHank Aaronhits Philadelphia PhilliesKen Brett's fastball for his 700th HR, in Atlanta
US House Judiciary approves two Articles of Impeachment against PresidentRichard Nixon
Billy Martinfired as Texas Rangers manager
NY Met Félix Millán hits 4 singles; erased by Joe Torres 4 double plays
Soviet spacecraftSoyuz 19returns to Earth after the historic 'handshake in space' with an Apollo spacecraft
"Sgt Pepper's Lonely Hearts Club Band" film starring TheBee Gees, based on the Beatles' album of the same name, premieres in NYC
Thunderstorm cuts shortDiana Ross's free concert in Central Park, New York City
Guns & Rosesdebut album "Appetite for Destruction" is released, and becomes one of the best-selling debut album of all time with more than 30 million copies sold
Massachusetts GovernorMichael Dukakisaccepts Democratic nomination for president
Mike TysonKOs Carl Williams in 1:33 for heavyweight boxing title

Pink Floyd's "The Wall" is performed where the Berlin Wall once stood in a charity concert led byRoger Waters, with guestsCyndi Lauper,Joni Mitchell, The Scorpions, Sinéad O'Connor,Bryan Adams, The Band,Van Morrison, The Hooters, and others
Ferguson Jenkins,Gaylord Perry,Rod Carew,Tony Lazzeri, andBill Veeck, Jr are inducted into the Baseball Hall of Fame in Cooperstown, New York
Tony Blairis declared the winner of the leadership election of the British Labour Party, paving the way for him to become Prime Minister in 1997
Wayne Gretzkysigns a 2-year deal with NY Rangers

With a record six races remaining, German Ferrari driverMichael Schumacherclinches his fifth F1 World Drivers' Championship with victory in the French Grand Prix at Circuit de Nevers Magny-Cours, his third straight title win
Harry Potter and the Deathly Hallows, the final book in the series by J.K. Rowling, is published worldwide, and 11 million copies sell in 24 hours
New Zealand beats Australia, 26-12 at Eden Park, Auckland to win their 3rd consecutive Tri Nations Rugby Series; flyhalfDan Carterlands 7 penalties for the All Blacks

Bosnian Serb politicianRadovan Karadžićis arrested in Serbia and indicted by the UN International Criminal Tribunal for the former Yugoslavia tribunal for genocide, crimes against humanity and war crimes during the 1995 Srebrenica massacre
Philippe of Belgiumbecomes King of the Belgiums after his father King Albert II's earlier abdication
"Guardians of the Galaxy", directed by James Gunn, starringChris Prattand Zoe Saldana, premieres in Hollywood, Los Angeles
Comedy series "Fleabag" premieres on the BBC starringPhoebe Waller-Bridge, Sian Clifford,Olivia Colman, andAndrew Scott
SingerJustin Bieberis barred from performing in China by Beijing Municipal Bureau of Culture, citing his "bad behavior"
Reboot of TV show "Buffy the Vampire Slayer" is announced with creatorJoss Whedonas executive producer (doesn't happen)
Marvel announces 10 new superhero films at Comic-Con, including Blade withMahershala Ali,Natalie Portmanas a female Thor, and the first Asian American superhero film, Shang-Chi
Former Sudanese leaderOmar al-Bashirgoes on trial in Khartoum for the 1989 coup that toppled the government
Stage 1 of the controversial filling of Blue Nile River dam announced completed by Ethiopian PMAbiy Ahmed
PresidentXi Jinpingarrives in Tibet in first official visit by a Chinese leader in 30 years
US PresidentJoe Bidentests positive for COVID-19
US PresidentJoe Bidenabandons his campaign for re-election and endorses Vice PresidentKamala Harris
United States Olympic and Paralympic Committee announces a ban on transgender women competing in Olympic women's sports, in compliance with an executive order issued byDonald Trump
John T. Scopesis found guilty of teaching evolution in the “Scopes monkey trial” in Dayton, Tennessee, fined $100 and costs
First polio case since 2013 reported in Rockford county, New York
